question: Do all wind-responsive elements (flag, smoke, trees, grass) consistently indicate a wind direction blowing from right to left?
Is the red flag on the lighthouse clearly streaming toward the left side of the image?
Is the smoke plume from the chimney drifting consistently toward the left?
Are the trees on the right side of the image leaning or showing wind stress toward the left?
Is the tall grass in the foreground bent toward the left, consistent with the new wind direction?
If rain streaks or sea spray are visible, are they angled or drifting in a direction consistent with wind blowing from right to left?
Are the static structures (lighthouse tower, lantern room, stone cottage) preserved without distortion or unwanted geometrical changes?
Is the rocky coastline and cliff geometry preserved intact?
Are the background elements (stormy ocean waves, overcast sky) preserved, excluding necessary adjustments for wind direction?
Are the original lighting, color grading, and key object attributes (e.g., the red color of the flag) preserved?
Is the image free of ghosting, blurring, or artifacts, particularly in areas where the flag and smoke were originally positioned?
Are the modified elements (flag, smoke, vegetation) rendered with realistic physics and seamless visual integration (no halos or jagged edges)?
